title: Build a WhatsApp AI Sales Agent for Any Local Business — Complete Setup Guide

i.c: Build a WhatsApp AI Sales Agent for Any Local Business — Complete Setup Guide

title: A fully autonomous AI-powered sales agent that runs on WhatsApp for a bakery business called
The Cake Shop
b
. The agent handles product inquiries, takes orders, negotiates prices, answers FAQs, switches between languages, and collects UPI payments — all without any human in the loop.

i.c: A fully autonomous AI-powered sales agent that runs on WhatsApp for a bakery business called
. The agent handles product inquiries, takes orders, negotiates prices, answers FAQs, switches between languages, and collects UPI payments — all without any human in the loop.

title: This guide walks you through building the exact same agent from scratch. You can customize it for any local business — restaurants, salons, florists, clinics, or any service-based business.

i.c: This guide walks you through building the exact same agent from scratch. You can customize it for any local business — restaurants, salons, florists, clinics, or any service-based business.

title: Important:
b
The temporary token expires every 24 hours. For production, set up a System User with a permanent token.

i.c: The temporary token expires every 24 hours. For production, set up a System User with a permanent token.

title: 1. System Prompt
b
— The complete personality, product catalog, pricing, business rules, sales flow, and formatting instructions for the agent.

i.c: — The complete personality, product catalog, pricing, business rules, sales flow, and formatting instructions for the agent.

title: generate_upi_payment
c
— Creates a UPI deep link for payment

i.c: — Creates a UPI deep link for payment

title: 3. Agentic Loop
b
— The
chat()
c
function that keeps calling Claude in a loop until all tool calls are resolved and a final text response is ready.

i.c: function that keeps calling Claude in a loop until all tool calls are resolved and a final text response is ready.

title: 4. Conversation Memory
b
— A dictionary that stores conversation history per phone number, so the agent remembers context across messages.

i.c: — A dictionary that stores conversation history per phone number, so the agent remembers context across messages.

title: System prompt contains everything
b

— The full menu, prices, business hours, delivery rules, size guide, customization options, FAQ answers, objection handling scripts, and formatting rules are all in the system prompt. This means the agent doesn't need a database.

i.c: — The full menu, prices, business hours, delivery rules, size guide, customization options, FAQ answers, objection handling scripts, and formatting rules are all in the system prompt. This means the agent doesn't need a database.

title: WhatsApp formatting
b
— The system prompt explicitly tells the agent to use WhatsApp-native formatting (
bold
i
italic
i
, • bullets) instead of markdown.

i.c: — The system prompt explicitly tells the agent to use WhatsApp-native formatting (

title: Language detection
b
— The agent is instructed to detect the customer's language and reply in the exact same language.

i.c: — The agent is instructed to detect the customer's language and reply in the exact same language.

title: Sales flow
b
— The agent follows a strict order: product → size → customization → name → delivery/pickup → address → date/time → order summary → payment.

i.c: — The agent follows a strict order: product → size → customization → name → delivery/pickup → address → date/time → order summary → payment.

title: 1. Webhook Verification (GET /webhook)
b
— When you register the webhook on Meta, they send a GET request with a challenge. Your server verifies the token matches and returns the challenge.

i.c: — When you register the webhook on Meta, they send a GET request with a challenge. Your server verifies the token matches and returns the challenge.

title: 2. Message Handling (POST /webhook)
b

— When a customer sends a message on WhatsApp, Meta sends a POST request to your webhook with the message payload. The server extracts the phone number and message text, passes it to the agent, and sends the agent's response back via the WhatsApp Cloud API.

i.c: — When a customer sends a message on WhatsApp, Meta sends a POST request to your webhook with the message payload. The server extracts the phone number and message text, passes it to the agent, and sends the agent's response back via the WhatsApp Cloud API.

title: Always return HTTP 200 to Meta, even if there's an error — otherwise Meta retries and you get duplicate messages

i.c: Always return HTTP 200 to Meta, even if there's an error — otherwise Meta retries and you get duplicate messages

title: The WhatsApp Cloud API URL format:
https://graph.facebook.com/v25.0/{PHONE_NUMBER_ID}/messages
c

i.c: Non-text messages (images, audio, stickers) get a polite fallback response

title: A simple script that loads .env variables and starts the Flask server on port 8000.

i.c: A simple script that loads .env variables and starts the Flask server on port 8000.

title:
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()
from whatsapp_bot import app
if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(port=8000, host="0.0.0.0", debug=False)

i.c:
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()
from whatsapp_bot import app
if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(port=8000, host="0.0.0.0", debug=False)
